carousel.title: What is a brain-computer interface?
carousel.body: A BCI reads neural signals from the brain and translates them into commands for an external device, here a robotic arm. No muscles are required: intent is decoded directly from cortical activity.

carousel.title: The motor homunculus
carousel.body: The primary motor cortex is organized as a "homunculus": a distorted body map on the precentral gyrus. Hand and face occupy large lateral areas; shoulder and trunk sit more superiorly toward the midline. The leg and foot are represented on the paracentral lobule, not the shoulder.

carousel.title: Primary vs. premotor cortex
carousel.body: M1 (primary motor cortex) on the precentral gyrus sends direct movement commands. Dorsal premotor cortex (PMd) plans reaching and shoulder orientation before execution. Premotor areas coordinate arm trajectories rather than hosting a single "elbow patch."

carousel.title: This is a live systems demo
carousel.body: Buttons stand in for decoded intent so you can explore the full stack: cortical mapping, motion commands, and the robot arm. In the full system, EEG replaces the click.

carousel.title: Braindecode and EEG CNNs
carousel.body: We train motor-intent models with Braindecode, a PyTorch library for EEG deep learning. Convolutional networks learn spatial and temporal patterns in scalp EEG that correspond to imagined or attempted movement.

carousel.title: ONNX and C++ inference
carousel.body: Trained models export to ONNX and run in a C++ inference pipeline for low-latency decoding on the edge. That keeps classification close to the robot control loop instead of round-tripping through Python at runtime.

carousel.title: EEG brain-to-text
carousel.body: Alongside motor control, we are building an EEG brain-to-text path: decode linguistic or communicative intent from neural signals and turn it into text. The same record-decode-act pattern, aimed at communication rather than the arm.

carousel.title: From thought to action
carousel.body: A typical BCI pipeline: record brain activity, extract features, classify intent, then send a motor command. This demo lets you trigger the act stage by hand while the decode stack is developed in parallel.

carousel.title: Supplementary motor area
carousel.body: The SMA, on the medial superior frontal cortex, supports movement initiation, sequencing, and coordinated multi-joint actions. In this demo it stands in for returning the arm to a home pose, not a literal "reset" circuit.

carousel.title: Why highlight the brain?
carousel.body: Each control maps to a region upper-limb BCIs often target: hand in M1, shoulder elevation in superior M1, reaching and orientation in PMd, arm coordination across M1 and premotor cortex, and sequenced poses in SMA.

carousel.title: Restoring movement
carousel.body: BCIs paired with robotic assistive devices aim to restore independence after spinal cord injury, stroke, or ALS by translating preserved cortical signals into useful limb motion.
